data.content:
### Specify Ready Command via CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/ready-cmd/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to set the ready command using the `e2b template build` CLI command with the `--ready-cmd` option. The command provided will be executed to determine sandbox readiness.

```bash
e2b template build --ready-cmd "<your-ready-command>"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Ready Command in e2b.toml

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/ready-cmd/page.mdx

This shows how to configure the ready command within the `e2b.toml` file. The `ready_cmd` field accepts a string representing the command to be executed for readiness checks.

```toml
# This is a config for E2B sandbox template
template_id = "1wdqsf9le9gk21ztb4mo"
dockerfile = "e2b.Dockerfile"
template_name = "my-agent-sandbox"
ready_cmd = "<your-ready-command>"
```

--------------------------------

### Ready Command Examples for E2B

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/ready-cmd/page.mdx

This section provides practical examples of ready commands for common scenarios. These include waiting for a specific HTTP status code, checking for a running process, or verifying the existence of a file.

```bash
ready_cmd = 'curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{{http_code}}" http://localhost:3000 | grep -q "200"'
```

```bash
ready_cmd = 'pgrep my-process-name > /dev/null'
```

```bash
ready_cmd = '[ -f /tmp/ready.flag ]'
```

--------------------------------

### Commands API - Run Command (Foreground)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the foreground and waits for it to finish executing.

```APIDOC
## POST /commands/run

### Description
Starts a new command in the foreground and waits for it to finish executing. Returns the command result upon completion.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/commands/run

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Request Body
- **cmd** (str) - Required - Command to execute.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables for the command.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - User to run the command as (default: "user").
- **cwd** (str) - Optional - Working directory for the command.
- **on_stdout** (Callable[[str], None]) - Optional - Callback function for command stdout output.
- **on_stderr** (Callable[[str], None]) - Optional - Callback function for command stderr output.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the command execution in seconds (0 for no limit, default: 60).
- **background** (Literal[False, None]) - Optional - Must be `False` or omitted to run in the foreground.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "cmd": "ls -l",
  "cwd": "/app",
  "on_stdout": "handle_stdout"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **result** (CommandResult) - The result of the command execution.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "result": {
    "stdout": "file1\nfile2",
    "stderr": "",
    "exit_code": 0
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Commands API - Run Command (Foreground)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the foreground and waits for its execution to complete. Returns the command's result.

```APIDOC
## POST /commands/run

### Description
Start a new command in the foreground and wait until it finishes executing. Returns the result of the command execution.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/commands/run

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Request Body
- **cmd** (str) - Required - The command to execute.
- **background** (Literal[False] | None) - Optional - Set to `False` or omit to run the command in the foreground.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables to set for the command.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - The user to run the command as. Defaults to "user".
- **cwd** (str) - Optional - The working directory for the command.
- **on_stdout** (OutputHandler[Stdout]) - Optional - A callback function to handle standard output streams.
- **on_stderr** (OutputHandler[Stderr]) - Optional - A callback function to handle standard error streams.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the command connection in seconds. Use `0` for no time limit. Defaults to 60 seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "cmd": "ls -l",
  "cwd": "/app",
  "envs": {"MY_VAR": "value"}
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **stdout** (str) - The standard output of the command.
- **stderr** (str) - The standard error of the command.
- **exit_code** (int) - The exit code of the command.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "stdout": "total 4\n-rw-r--r-- 1 user user 0 Jan  1 12:00 file.txt",
  "stderr": "",
  "exit_code": 0
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command Options - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for starting a new command within the sandbox environment. This includes configuration for running commands in the background, setting the working directory, defining environment variables, and providing callbacks for standard output and error streams. It also includes options for request and command timeouts, and the user to run the command as.

```typescript
background?: boolean;
cwd?: string;
envs?: Record<string, string>;
onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
requestTimeoutMs?: number;
timeoutMs?: number;
user?: Username;
```

--------------------------------

### Commands API - Run Command (Background)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the background and returns a handle to interact with it.

```APIDOC
## POST /commands/run

### Description
Starts a new command in the background and returns a handle to interact with it. Use the handle to manage the command execution.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/commands/run

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Request Body
- **cmd** (str) - Required - Command to execute.
- **background** (Literal[True]) - Required - Must be `True` to run in the background.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables for the command.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - User to run the command as (default: "user").
- **cwd** (str) - Optional - Working directory for the command.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the command execution in seconds (0 for no limit, default: 60).

### Request Example
```json
{
  "cmd": "sleep 60",
  "background": true
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **handle** (CommandHandle) - A handle to interact with the background command.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "handle": {
    "pid": 456
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Commands API - Run Command (Background)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.4/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the background and returns a handle to interact with it.

```APIDOC
## POST /commands/run

### Description
Start a new command in the background and return a handle that can be used to interact with the running command.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/commands/run

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

#### Request Body
- **cmd** (str) - Required - The command to execute.
- **background** (Literal[True]) - Required - Set to `True` to run the command in the background.
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables to set for the command.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - The user to run the command as. Defaults to "user".
- **cwd** (str) - Optional - The working directory for the command.
- **on_stdout** (OutputHandler[Stdout]) - Optional - A callback function to handle standard output streams.
- **on_stderr** (OutputHandler[Stderr]) - Optional - A callback function to handle standard error streams.
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the command connection in seconds. Use `0` for no time limit. Defaults to 60 seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "cmd": "sleep 10",
  "background": true
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **handle** (AsyncCommandHandle) - An object that provides methods to interact with the background command (e.g., `kill()`, `wait()`).

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "command_id": "cmd-abc-123"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Commands API - Run Command (Foreground)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Starts a new command in the foreground and waits for its execution to complete. Returns the result of the command.

```APIDOC
## POST /commands/run/foreground

### Description
Start a new command and wait until it finishes executing.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/commands/run/foreground

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

#### Request Body
- **cmd** (str) - Required - Command to execute
- **envs** (Dict[str, str]) - Optional - Environment variables used for the command
- **user** (Username) - Optional - User to run the command as (default: "user")
- **cwd** (str) - Optional - Working directory to run the command
- **on_stdout** (Callable[[str], None]) - Optional - Callback for command stdout output
- **on_stderr** (Callable[[str], None]) - Optional - Callback for command stderr output
- **timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the command connection in seconds. Using `0` will not limit the command connection time (default: 60)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **result** (CommandResult) - Result of the command execution
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Command Start Options in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.3/commands/page.mdx

Defines the options for starting a new command in the E2B environment. This includes settings for running commands in the background, specifying the working directory (cwd), environment variables (envs), and callbacks for standard error (onStderr) and standard output (onStdout). It also supports request and command timeouts, and specifying the user to run the command as.

```ts
{
  background?: boolean;
  cwd?: string;
  envs?: Record<string, string>;
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Set Command User

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies the user under which a command should be executed. This option is part of the CommandStartOpts interface and defaults to the user who started the command.

```typescript
optional user: Username;

// Default
// user
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template with Start Command (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/start-cmd/page.mdx

Specifies a command to be executed when the sandbox is spawned. This is useful for pre-loading data or starting services. The command is passed using the `-c` flag during the template build process.

```bash
e2b template build -c "<your-start-command>"
```
